verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone retained by the KDEL signal and lacks a classical TM domain. However, under ER stress (especially in cancer/hypoxia), it translocates to the outer face of the plasma membrane via its own mechanism (a subpopulation behaves as an ER transmembrane protein that cycles to the PM). Surface GRP78 is documented across AML, GBM, lung, pancreatic, and breast cancers and is restricted intracellularly in normal cells. Multiple CAR-T, antibody, and peptide-drug programs directly target cell-surface GRP78 in preclinical and clinical settings, confirming genuine extracellular accessibility in the stress/cancer cell state.
